content.boundary: The rationale for a national framework rests on documented gaps (no threshold, no registry, training mismatches, subsidy-cost mismatch) rather than direct evidence that such a framework would work in Hungary, since no comparable Hungarian pilot exists.
content.change_lever: No bright-line statutory minimum enrollment threshold currently exists in Hungarian education law, unlike comparative models such as Ukraine's 45-student rule; closure decisions operate on a discretionary, case-by-case basis, creating unpredictability that a transparent national threshold framework would address.; Given that the curated source registry available for this topic contains no equivalent Hungarian closure/outcome dataset and multiple experts independently note the absence of systematic tracking, the claim that 'no current national registry of closure decisions and outcomes exists in Hungary' is a reasonable inference from an absence of evidence rather than a directly documented negative finding.
content.name: National systemic framework: transparency, thresholds and evidence infrastructure
content.system_problem: Establish a national framework of transparent minimum-viability thresholds, cost-visibility rules, teacher-training standardization, and a closure/outcome registry to inform (not dictate) local decisions across any of the above models.
